Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Pharmacists, nurses, physicians, clinical assistants, pharmacy technicians, medical assistants, healthcare administrators, hospital staff
Team or Dept this is for: Pharmacy, Clinical Services, Patient Safety, Quality Assurance, Healthcare Operations, Medical Affairs
Description: When AI misses dangerous combinations or gives incomplete advice.
Prerequisites: A basic understanding of common medications and their general uses, along with familiarity with everyday pharmacy or clinical workflows, is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify common scenarios where AI tools may miss dangerous drug combinations and explain the limitations that contribute to incomplete or inaccurate interaction advice. You will also apply practical strategies to verify AI-generated recommendations and recognise when to escalate concerns to a qualified healthcare or pharmacy professional.
